name: route-lazy
title: Route declaration with loadComponent (lazy)
description: Pattern for declaring a route in apps/*/src/app/app.routes.ts that lazy-loads its component. All non-shell routes use loadComponent. Pure pattern entry — no component source.

cena.whenToUse: Whenever adding a new route that points to a component which is not in the eager-loaded shell. All feature-route components should be lazy-loaded.

parameters.path: Route URL segment, no leading slash
parameters.relative-path: Relative path from app.routes.ts to the component file (no leading './')
parameters.guards: Optional comma-separated list of functional guards (see route-guard pattern)

gotchas: Path strings without leading slash; the router resolves relative to the parent route
loadComponent's import path is relative to the routes file, not absolute
Use .then(m => m.X) form, not .then(({X}) => X) — easier to spot the exported class name in PR review

preferenceLedgerRows.name: Angular 21.2.13
preferenceLedgerRows.applies: loadComponent is the standalone way since Angular 17; we are on 21.x
